skill.name: create-auth-skill
skill.description: Guide for adding Better Auth to TypeScript and JavaScript applications with framework setup, database adapters, plugins, and security checks.
skill.summary: Create Better Auth implementations for modern TypeScript and JavaScript apps.
skill.author: better-auth

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many patterns, but review shows SKILL.md is a Markdown implementation guide with inline commands, package names, URLs, environment variable placeholders, and cookie terminology. No executable scripts, prompt injection text, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious network behavior were found; residual risk is limited to users following CLI and configuration guidance.

low_findings.title: Documentation Includes CLI Commands
low_findings.description: SKILL.md recommends package installation, secret generation, and database migration commands. These are legitimate Better Auth setup steps, but users should review commands before running them because migrations can change project files or databases.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible Markdown examples and are not executed by the skill. They still represent real user-directed external commands with project impact.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Placeholders
low_findings.description: The guide shows BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, BETTER_AUTH_URL, DATABASE_URL, and OAuth secret names. This is appropriate configuration guidance and does not expose real secrets, but it reminds users to handle credentials carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are placeholders and variable names, not actual credentials. The finding is low risk because these names are expected for authentication setup.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to Better Auth documentation, GitHub examples, plugin documentation, CLI documentation, and migration guides. These are normal reference links with no evidence of exfiltration or hidden network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are explicit documentation resources matching the skill purpose. No code sends data to these locations.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive-Data Terms Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Scanner hits for browser credential files, Windows SAM, and weak cryptography map to benign auth terminology such as SAML, cookies, secure cookies, passkeys, and migration guides. No evidence found of credential file access, SAM access, weak cryptographic implementation, or prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the flagged lines are documentation text, not code accessing sensitive stores. Confidence is high because the file contains no executable implementation.

content.user_title: Build Better Auth for TypeScript Apps
content.value_statement: Teams need reliable authentication without missing framework, database, or security steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Better Auth setup and review.
actual_capabilities: Selects setup steps for new projects, existing projects, and migrations.
Outlines Better Auth server and client configuration files.
Maps route handler setup for Next.js, Express, SvelteKit, SolidStart, and Hono.
Lists database migration commands for Kysely, Prisma, and Drizzle workflows.
Summarizes common plugins for 2FA, organizations, admin features, passkeys, SSO, and API tokens.
Provides a security checklist for production authentication settings.
limitations: It is a guide, not an executable installer or generated application.
It relies on current Better Auth documentation for exact API details.
It does not validate real OAuth provider settings or database credentials.
It does not replace a security review of the final application.

use_cases.title: Add Auth to a New App
use_cases.description: Use the decision tree to choose framework files, database setup, client exports, route handlers, and initial migration steps.

use_cases.title: Upgrade an Existing Login Flow
use_cases.description: Plan an incremental Better Auth migration while checking current gaps, provider support, session handling, and account linking.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Security
use_cases.description: Review secret handling, secure cookies, trusted origins, rate limits, email verification, password reset, and 2FA requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new app
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-auth-skill guide to plan Better Auth setup for my new TypeScript app. Ask me for the framework and database first.

prompt_templates.title: Add Auth to Existing Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my project structure and use the create-auth-skill guide to add Better Auth with minimal changes. Identify files to create or update.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Plugins and Migrations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-auth-skill guide to add 2FA, organizations, and passkeys. Explain the server plugins, client plugins, and migration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-auth-skill security checklist to audit my Better Auth implementation for production readiness. Prioritize actionable risks.

output_examples.input: Plan Better Auth for a Next.js app with Prisma.
output: A framework-specific setup plan for server and client auth files.
Route handler placement for the Next.js App Router.
A Prisma migration sequence and reminders to rerun generation after plugin changes.
A production checklist covering secrets, secure cookies, trusted origins, and rate limits.

output_examples.input: Add social login and 2FA to an existing app.
output: A staged integration plan for social providers and two-factor authentication.
Required environment variable names without exposing secret values.
Plugin placement guidance for server and client configuration.
Testing checkpoints for sign-in, session retrieval, password reset, and redirect behavior.

output_examples.input: Review my auth setup before launch.
output: A prioritized launch review of cookie security, trusted origins, email verification, and rate limits.
Warnings for missing 2FA or weak account linking controls in sensitive applications.
Follow-up questions for OAuth redirect URIs, database migrations, and session expiration settings.

best_practices: Confirm the framework, database, and deployment URL before generating configuration steps.
Use placeholders for secrets and ask the user to provide values through their normal secret manager.
Run migrations in a development environment before applying them to production data.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real OAuth secrets or database URLs into prompts.
Do not skip secure cookies, trusted origins, or rate limits for production deployments.
Do not run generated migrations against production before reviewing the schema changes.

faq.question: Does this skill install Better Auth automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup guidance and command recommendations for an AI coding assistant to apply with user approval.

faq.question: Which frameworks are covered?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, Solid, Express, SvelteKit, SolidStart, Hono, and vanilla JavaScript clients.

faq.question: Does it support database migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines Better Auth migration or generation commands for built-in Kysely, Prisma, and Drizzle workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with enterprise auth?
faq.answer: Yes. It references SSO, SCIM, organizations, admin features, and account linking review for enterprise use cases.

faq.question: Should I provide real secrets to the assistant?
faq.answer: No. Use placeholder names in prompts and store real values in your secret manager or deployment environment.

faq.question: Is a security review still required?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide includes a checklist, but the final application still needs review and testing before production release.
